title: Soy Sauce on Linen
timeEstimate: 15–40 minutes for fresh washable fabric; aged or heat-set stains may need professional review
stainChemistry: Treat soy sauce on linen conservatively as a dark food/beverage/tannin-adjacent spill. The available packet supports family-level food/beverage/tannin-adjacent handling, not soy-specific salt mordant, melanoidin, caramel, amino-acid, or timing chemistry claims.
whyThisWorks: Blotting and back-rinsing reduce loose, water-soluble spill material before it spreads deeper. Generic liquid/heavy-duty laundry detergent helps suspend remaining residue, while care-label laundering and air-dry inspection avoid heat-setting any residual brown color.
defaultAssumption: Use only for washable, simple, unstructured linen. Dry-clean-only, structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen escalates before wet Home treatment.

spottingProtocol.technique: Care-label, construction, fiber, and colorfastness assessment
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cloth, hidden seam/hem test
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Stop immediately on transfer or change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Confirm the linen item is safe for professional wet-side treatment and document Home attempts. Treat soy sauce only within source-supported family guidance, not unsupported exact chemistry claims.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only intake. Follow care label; reject or escalate unstable dye, structured construction, or high-value textiles. Use PPE/ventilation required by any product SDS.

spottingProtocol.technique: Use the mildest source-supported professional path first
spottingProtocol.equipment: Product-labeled detergent or family-specific professional spotter; clean towels
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Use product-label dwell only; stop on dye movement, ring formation, or texture change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Begin with controlled water/detergent action for soy sauce. Any family-specific professional spotter must remain Pro-only, label-directed, and bounded by fabric test results.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Do not expose Home users to pro solvents, acids, oxidizers, concentrated spotters, steam, or exact percentages. Use SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, and do not mix incompatible chemistry.

spottingProtocol.technique: Flush between products and inspect dry
spottingProtocol.equipment: Water/steam only if fabric and product label permit; drying rack
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Until residues are flushed and fabric is dry enough to inspect
spottingProtocol.instruction: Flush or rinse out chemistry before switching products. Air-dry/inspect before considering any heat, oxidizer, solvent, or additional professional escalation.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Do not mix incompatible chemistry; use PPE/ventilation and document any last-resort escalation.

products: Product-labeled professional detergent/family-specific spotting systems only, used per SDS/label
professionalProtocol.temperature: Cool/cold first; any warmer professional step only after care-label review, testing, and chemistry removal controls
warnings: Pro-only chemistry requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, hidden-area testing, and flush/rinse/neutralization controls.
Do not expose Home users to professional-strength acids, solvents, oxidizers, named pro products, exact concentrations, steam, or heat escalation.

homeSolutions.instruction: Check the care label and construction. If the linen is dry-clean-only, structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, or dye-unstable, stop before wet treatment and use a professional.
Blot liquid or lift excess with a clean white cloth. Do not rub.
Hold the back of the stained area under cool or cold running water if the care label allows.
Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent. Use only product-label directions and test colored fabric on a hidden seam or hem first.
Wash according to the care label using cool, cold, or care-label-safe water. Air-dry completely and inspect before any dryer or iron.

homeSolutions.agent: Repeat Once If Improving or Escalate
homeSolutions.instruction: If the stain is clearly improving, repeat this conservative water-and-detergent path once. Escalate if color remains, dye bleeds, a ring or oily halo spreads, texture changes, the stain was heat-dried/ironed, or there is no improvement after two rounds.

steps: 1. Care Label and Colorfastness Gate: Check the care label and construction. If the linen is dry-clean-only, structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, or dye-unstable, stop before wet treatment and use a professional.
2. Blot or Lift Excess: Blot liquid or lift excess with a clean white cloth. Do not rub.
3. Cool/Cold Back-Rinse If Washable: Hold the back of the stained area under cool or cold running water if the care label allows.

4. Generic Liquid/Heavy-Duty Laundry Detergent: Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent. Use only product-label directions and test colored fabric on a hidden seam or hem first.
5. Care-Label Wash, Then Air-Dry: Wash according to the care label using cool, cold, or care-label-safe water. Air-dry completely and inspect before any dryer or iron.

6. Repeat Once If Improving or Escalate: If the stain is clearly improving, repeat this conservative water-and-detergent path once. Escalate if color remains, dye bleeds, a ring or oily halo spreads, texture changes, the stain was heat-dried/ironed, or there is no improvement after two rounds.
products: Generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent
diyProtocol.whenToStop: If the stain is clearly improving, repeat this conservative water-and-detergent path once. Escalate if color remains, dye bleeds, a ring or oily halo spreads, texture changes, the stain was heat-dried/ironed, or there is no improvement after two rounds.
warnings: No dryer, iron, or heat until the stain is gone after air-drying inspection
No Home vinegar/acids, peroxide, chlorine bleach, pro spotters, solvents, sunlight/UV claims, or hot-wash default

neverDo: Never put the item in a dryer, use an iron, or apply heat until the stain is gone after air-drying inspection
Never rub or scrub aggressively; blot, lift, scrape, or gently work only enough to avoid driving soil deeper
Never mix cleaning agents, bleach, ammonia, acids, solvents, or professional spotters
Never use chlorine bleach unless the care label allows it and the fabric is white or confirmed colorfast
Never use professional-strength oxidizers, acids, dry-side solvents, rust/mineral removers, adhesive removers, exact pro percentages, or named pro products in the Home path
Never wet-treat dry-clean-only, structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen at Home
Never use chlorine bleach or hot water/high heat as a default on linen
fiberSensitivities: Linen is cellulose/flax; use this Home path only for washable, simple, unstructured linen
Colored linen needs a hidden hem/white-cloth colorfastness test before detergent dwell, acids/oxidizers, or warmer water
Avoid abrasion, over-wetting structured linen, strong alkali, prolonged acids, chlorine, and high heat unless a professional has tested and accepted the risk
chemicalBoundaries: Home path is limited to cool/cold or care-label-safe lukewarm water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering
Use detergent or pretreat only according to its product label and only after a hidden-area test on colored fabric

Oxidizers are not routine Home steps in this repair; any future trace-color escalation must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, never mixed with other cleaners, and separately sourced
Professional chemistry is Pro-only and requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse/neutralization controls
chemicalControls: Home path is limited to water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering

No vinegar/acids, peroxide, chlorine bleach, dry-side solvent, professional tannin spotter, neutral synthetic detergent product claim, branded cleaner claim, or exact pro concentration in the Home path
Professional chemistry is Pro-only with SDS/label, PPE/ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse/neutralization controls
homeAllowed: Blot, lift, or gently scrape excess before adding water
Cool/cold water or care-label-safe lukewarm water
Generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent
proOnly: Professional spotters, dry-side solvents, acids, oxidizers, reducers, concentrated chemistry, steam, and heat escalation require SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, flush/rinse/neutralization controls, and documented fabric testing
testFirst: Test detergent, warmer water, enzyme detergent, oxidizer escalation, acid spotter, solvent, or any pro chemistry on a hidden seam/hem with a clean white cloth before treating the visible area
safetyMatrix.colorfastnessTest: On a hidden seam or hem, press a clean white cloth with the planned detergent/water mix for one minute; stop if dye transfers, color changes, or texture changes.
safetyMatrix.flushNeutralizeDry: Rinse or flush out each cleaning agent before changing products; air-dry fully and inspect before any dryer, iron, heat, or professional escalation.
safetyMatrix.temperatureLimits: Cool/cold first for tannin-adjacent, beverage, unknown, or mixed food stains. Use care-label-safe lukewarm/wash temperature only after initial lift/rinse. No dryer, iron, or heat until clean.
escalationTriggers: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, odor after air-drying, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, dye bleed/color loss, texture change, ring formation, old/heat-set stain, or no improvement after one or two conservative rounds
stopAndEscalate: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, odor after air-drying, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, dye bleed/color loss, texture change, ring formation, old/heat-set stain, or no improvement after one or two conservative rounds
safetyMatrix.ppeVentilation: For Home detergent use, follow the product label and keep products away from children and pets. For concentrates or Pro chemistry, use gloves/eye protection as the SDS requires and work with ventilation.
safetyMatrix.homeVsProBoundary: Home guidance excludes professional-strength oxidizers, acids, solvents, named pro products, exact pro concentrations, steam, and heat escalation; those belong only in a professional workflow with SDS/label controls.

materialWarnings: Washable, simple linen only; structured/lined/vintage/high-value/dry-clean-only linen escalates before wet treatment
Start cool/cold and avoid dryer/iron/heat until the stain is gone
Home path uses generic detergent only; no vinegar/acids, peroxide, chlorine bleach, solvents, sunlight/UV, branded cleaners, or pro spotters

escalation.when: Escalate structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, dye bleed/color loss, texture change, ring formation, old/heat-set stain, or no improvement after one or two conservative rounds
escalation.whatToTell: Soy Sauce on Linen; age of stain; care label/fabric color; whether cool/cold water and generic detergent were used; whether dye transfer, ring/halo, odor, heat exposure, or prior Home chemistry occurred.
escalation.specialistType: professional textile cleaner or dry cleaner

contextQuestions: Has it been through hot water, a dryer, or an iron?
Is the linen washable and colorfast?
Was any detergent, bleach, acid, peroxide, solvent, or branded cleaner already used?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Use only for washable, simple, unstructured linen. Dry-clean-only, structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen escalates before wet Home treatment.

sources.title: University Stain Science (compiled extension reference)
sources.evidence: Consumer stain-family guidance: cold/cool first for protein/unknown stains, tannin and combination stain families, liquid detergent/lukewarm fallback, blot/do not rub, work from the wrong side where appropriate, test first, and do not iron or dry until the stain is removed.

sources.title: EPA Alternative Cleaning Curriculum
sources.evidence: Textile safety/process gates: heat, alkalinity, oxidation, concentration, water/heat mismanagement and rubbing can damage textiles; detergents/surfactants suspend soils; hidden-area testing, product-label following, PPE/ventilation, no incompatible mixes, care-label cleaning and reject/escalate gates.

sources.title: GONR Material Safety Matrix v2
sources.evidence: Internal cotton/linen safety boundaries, enzyme/protein/oil/tannin/oxidizer controls, colored-fabric testing, no hot water on protein stains, and never-mix chlorine/acids/ammonia safety.

sources.title: R.R. Street 4Streets On-the-Spot
sources.evidence: Professional family classification for food/beverage, wet-side tannin/sugar, and sauce/combination stains; does not support soy-specific salt chemistry.

sources.title: American Drycleaner Spotting Techniques
sources.evidence: Trade tannin/protein examples and pro tannin cautions including color testing and flushing; pro-only source.
